news.title: Meta layoffs starting this week stress harsh AI reality inside Zuckerberg’s company
news.summary: Meta is starting layoffs this week, with 8,000 jobs expected to be cut. Employees brace for a new era of AI at the company.

news.title: U.S. creates $1.7B 'lawfare' fund in exchange for Trump dropping $10B IRS suit
news.summary: Trump sued the IRS in late January over the leak of his tax information by an IRS employee Charles "Chaz" Littlejohn in 2019 and 2020.

news.title: The Fed will have to raise interest rates in July to appease 'bond vigilantes,' Yardeni says
news.summary: Kevin Warsh was sent to the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates. He may have to push for higher levels instead. Warsh is expected to be the next chair of the Fed.

news.title: 10-year Treasury yield touches highest in a year, Japan's 30-year yield rises to a record
news.summary: Global bond yields rise on Monday as fears of resurgent inflation grip financial markets. Global bond yields rose on Monday amid fears of resurgence in inflation.

news.title: Trump ballroom money in question after Senate parliamentarian rules. Thune says GOP will persist
news.summary: Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., said the GOP will refine its approach to seeking money for President Donald Trump's White House ballroom.

news.title: 5 money moves that made this 32-year-old dad a millionaire in just 9 years
news.summary: Blake Edwards and his wife became millionaires by their early 30s. A low starting salary, global pandemic, unaffordable housing market, graduate school and two kids didn't stop them.

news.title: Ford takes its first big step toward being an energy business
news.summary: Ford just inked a deal with a French electric-utility company. Ford has been working with the French company for more than a year. The two companies will work together to develop electric vehicles.

news.title: The oil market is reaching a ‘tipping point’ that could create problems for stocks, according to this Wall Street legend
news.summary: Evercore’s Roger Altman said a sharp rise in crude could destabilize markets before it may trigger “the second big inflation shock of this decade after COVID,” especially if oil prices climb toward $150 a barrel or higher.

news.title: Arm’s stock could rise another 45% as the ‘renaissance of CPUs’ takes hold, analyst says
news.summary: The market for server CPUs could quadruple to $137 billion by the end of the decade, according to Bernstein. Arm is destined to play a starring role in the market.

news.title: Are you ‘doomjobbing’ while looking for work? Know the signs — and do this instead.
news.summary: One job hunter only found work once she stopped making this popular application mistake. “It was soul-crushing,” says one job hunter.
